message.content:
You are verifier #1, independent of all other verifiers. Do NOT confirm findings optimistically — only confirm if you can independently verify the issue exists in the actual code at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay.

Finding F-038: "test_data_sovereignty_is_qualified_for_high_stakes_tier uses loose "off-box" keyword — one-sided guard"
File: tests/test_docs_drift.py (line 25)
Description: Test asserts "off-box" in text for GOVERNANCE.md. If high-stakes caveat deleted while another "off-box" occurrence remains, test passes. Also test_baseline_toolkit_scope_is_honest never asserts stale embedding/clustering claims are absent.
PoC: Remove only high-stakes caveat from GOVERNANCE.md (keep SaaS observability sentence). Test passes. Add stale embedding builders back alongside BaselineBuilder in ARCHITECTURE.md. Test passes.

Instructions:
1. Read the actual file at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/tests/test_docs_drift.py
2. Look at the code at line 25 and surrounding context
3. Independently verify whether the described problem EXISTS in the current code
4. Check if the PoC is reproducible from the actual code as written
5. Consider whether this is already fixed (pass-1 #101-#122, pass-2 #124-#156 fixed many issues)
6. Return your independent verdict

Confirmed = true ONLY if: (a) the code problem exists as described, (b) the PoC is reproducible from current code, (c) it is a genuine production-readiness concern. Set confirmed=false for speculative issues, issues already fixed, or issues where the PoC does not match what the code actually does.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
1	"""Docs-vs-code drift guards (pass-2 #136, #150, #151, #152, #153, #154).
2	
3	Normative docs make claims a public reader relies on. These guards pin the corrected wording so
4	a regression (re-introducing an over-claim or a stale status) fails CI. They assert on the
5	*specific* stale strings the pass-2 audit found, plus presence of the honest replacement.
6	"""
7	
8	from __future__ import annotations
9	
10	from pathlib import Path
11	
12	_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent
13	
14	
15	def _read(rel: str) -> str:
16	    return (_ROOT / rel).read_text(encoding="utf-8")
17	
18	
19	def test_data_sovereignty_is_qualified_for_high_stakes_tier():
20	    # #136: the absolute "nothing leaves the machine" over-claim must be qualified — the optional
21	    # high-stakes tier sends prompt content off-box.
22	    for rel in ("README.md", "docs/CHARTER.md", "docs/GOVERNANCE.md"):
23	        text = _read(rel)
24	        assert "nothing leaves the machine" not in text, f"{rel}: unconditional over-claim (#136)"
25	        assert "off-box" in text, f"{rel}: high-stakes off-box caveat missing (#136)"
26	    adr = _read("docs/decisions/ADR-0003-local-first-data-sovereign.md")
27	    assert "off-box" in adr and "the same as" in adr  # billing vs residency separated
28	
29	
30	def test_charter_status_is_not_stale():
31	    # #150: the status line must not still call the seams "stubs being wired".
32	    charter = _read("docs/CHARTER.md")
33	    assert "stubs being wired" not in charter
34	    assert "implemented and composed" in charter or "in place and hardened" in charter
35	
36	
37	def test_baseline_toolkit_scope_is_honest():
38	    # #151: the engine ships primitives + a determinism harness, NOT embedding/clustering/graph
39	    # builders — those are adapter-supplied. Docs/package docstring must say so.
40	    arch = _read("docs/ARCHITECTURE.md")
41	    init = _read("src/assay_engine/__init__.py")
42	    for text in (arch, init):
43	        # the corrected text names the adapter's BaselineBuilder as the source of choice-bearing
44	        # builders rather than claiming the engine ships them
45	        assert "BaselineBuilder" in text
46	
47	
48	def test_preregistration_timestamp_wording_matches_shipped_default():
49	    # #152: body text must not present RFC-3161 as the in-force mechanism; HMAC is the default.
50	    for rel in ("docs/METHODOLOGY.md", "docs/GOVERNANCE.md"):
51	        text = _read(rel)
52	        assert "HMAC" in text, f"{rel}: shipped HMAC default not stated (#152)"
53	        assert "pluggable" in text, f"{rel}: RFC-3161 must be described as pluggable (#152)"
54	    # Guard the SPECIFIC stale over-claims, so re-introducing either one fails CI even though the
55	    # word "HMAC" appears elsewhere in the file (file-level substring checks alone don't catch a
56	    # localized regression — the pass-2 confirmation flagged this on GOVERNANCE §2 step 3).
57	    gov = _read("docs/GOVERNANCE.md")
58	    assert "RFC-3161 timestamped** against a trusted timestamp authority" not in gov, (
59	        "GOVERNANCE §2 step 3 stale RFC-3161 over-claim reintroduced (#152)"
60	    )
61	    meth = _read("docs/METHODOLOGY.md")
62	    assert "RFC-3161 timestamped before confirmation" not in meth, (
63	        "METHODOLOGY §7 stale RFC-3161 over-claim reintroduced (#152)"
64	    )
65	
66	
67	def test_readme_install_notes_not_on_pypi():
68	    # #153: the quickstart must not present `pip install assay-engine` as a working path without
69	    # the not-yet-on-PyPI caveat + a from-source path.
70	    readme = _read("README.md")
71	    assert "Not yet on PyPI" in readme
72	    assert "pip install -e" in readme  # the working from-source path
73	
74	
75	def test_adr0006_lists_the_baseline_extra():
76	    # #154: ADR-0006's extras enumeration must include `baseline` (pyproject has five extras).
77	    adr = _read("docs/decisions/ADR-0006-optional-backends-lazy-imports.md")
78	    assert "`baseline`" in adr
79

125	## 5. Data sovereignty
126	
127	All computation, storage, tracing, experiment tracking, and the local **bulk** reasoning tier
128	run **on-box** — no data, embeddings, traces, or results from those paths leave the machine.
129	SaaS observability and any service that would ship analyzed content off-box are disqualified by
130	this constraint, independent of cost. The one deliberate exception is the optional **high-stakes**
131	reasoning tier, which sends prompt content to a frontier model via a fixed-cost subscription
132	(not metered, but off-box); a study handling data that must never leave the box uses only the
133	bulk tier. This is a billing-vs-residency distinction: "no metered API" is not the same as
134	"on-box".
135	
136	## 6. Reproducibility guarantees (summary)
137	
138	- Deterministic seeds (documented); inputs hashed; model names + versions recorded.
139	- Append-only provenance for every action.

content.text:
Now I have enough information to assess the finding. Let me analyze:

1. In GOVERNANCE.md, "off-box" appears at lines 129 and 132. Line 129 is about SaaS observability (disqualified services), and line 132 is specifically in the context of the high-stakes tier exception. Both are in the same paragraph, so the high-stakes caveat and the SaaS "off-box" mention are intertwined.

2. The test at line 25 simply does `assert "off-box" in text` — a file-level substring check. If someone deleted the entire high-stakes caveat paragraph (lines 130-132) but left line 129 ("SaaS observability and any service that would ship analyzed content off-box are disqualified"), the test would still pass even though the high-stakes tier exception caveat is gone.

3. For `test_baseline_toolkit_scope_is_honest`, the test only asserts `"BaselineBuilder" in text` — it never asserts that stale embedding/clustering builder names are absent. Adding `EmbeddingBuilder` or `ClusteringBuilder` back to ARCHITECTURE.md alongside `BaselineBuilder` would not be caught.

The PoC is valid: the test at line 25 is a loose one-sided guard ("off-box" must exist somewhere in the file), and the `test_baseline_toolkit_scope_is_honest` test has no negative assertions to catch stale embedding/clustering claims returning.

input.reasoning:
The code problems exist exactly as described.

In test_data_sovereignty_is_qualified_for_high_stakes_tier (line 25 of /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/tests/test_docs_drift.py), the assertion is simply `assert "off-box" in text` — a file-level substring match. In GOVERNANCE.md, "off-box" appears at line 129 (describing disqualified SaaS services) and line 132 (in the high-stakes tier caveat). If a developer deleted lines 130-134 (the high-stakes caveat block) while leaving line 129 intact ("SaaS observability and any service that would ship analyzed content off-box are disqualified"), the test would still pass despite the high-stakes exception being removed. The PoC is reproducible because the SaaS disqualification sentence at line 129 uses "off-box" independently of the high-stakes caveat.

In test_baseline_toolkit_scope_is_honest (line 37-45), the test only asserts `"BaselineBuilder" in text` for both ARCHITECTURE.md and the package __init__.py. There are no negative assertions preventing stale embedding/clustering/graph builder names from coexisting with BaselineBuilder. Re-adding EmbeddingBuilder or ClusteringBuilder references alongside BaselineBuilder would not fail CI.

These are genuine one-sided guard weaknesses: the tests can pass even when the specific documentation claims they are supposed to protect against are violated. Neither issue appears to have been fixed in the current code.
